rollingHandoffIn:
Speaker registry (reuse speaker_id only for the same acoustic voice):
- spk_001 => Speaker 1 | grounded confidence: 0.90 | acoustic cues: dramatic, cinematic narration style | inferred traits: gender: female | recent lines: But we are not afraid. / Menendez is a Terrorist. We're bringing peace and security to the world.
- spk_002 => Speaker 2 | grounded confidence: 0.85 | acoustic cues: menacing, gravelly, deep tone | inferred traits: gender: male | recent lines: So eager to leave, are we? / Kill them, men.
- spk_003 => Speaker 3 | grounded confidence: 0.90 | acoustic cues: authoritative, news-anchor tone | inferred traits: gender: male | recent lines: Raul Menendez ignited global unrest on an unprecedented scale.
- spk_004 => Speaker 4 | grounded confidence: 0.85 | acoustic cues: distressed, intimate tone | inferred traits: gender: male | recent lines: He refuses to let me go. / This isn't real. The hell it is.
- spk_005 => Speaker 5 | grounded confidence: 0.90 | acoustic cues: authoritative, tactical, gravelly | inferred traits: gender: male | recent lines: Spectre 1, report. / Pull it together, man!
- spk_006 => Speaker 6 | grounded confidence: 0.85 | acoustic cues: radio-filtered, urgent | inferred traits: gender: male | recent lines: Need a sitrep.
- spk_007 => Speaker 7 | grounded confidence: 0.85 | acoustic cues: chanting, processed, rhythmic | recent lines: Master of the invisible, master of the space. Master of your mind, master of your fate. / Master, master, wins and brings it all in after. Master, master, we will be the ones to cry.

Voice separation reminders:
- speaker_id continuity is acoustic, not semantic. A speaker naming another person is not evidence they are that person.
- Do not merge narration/figurehead VO, antagonist taunts, female radio/comms, gruff soldier responses, and promo-announcer copy unless the voice itself clearly matches.
- If delivery shifts from direct character/threat speech into official public-address, newsreel, briefing, or expository narration, prefer a new speaker_id unless the exact same voice clearly continues.
- If adjacent words are one uninterrupted utterance from the same voice, keep them together instead of splitting them into artificial fragments.
- The handoff is reference-only memory. Never copy or continue prior lines unless they are audibly present in THIS chunk.
- If the current chunk only contains one audible promo/narration line, return only that line; do not invent follow-up tactical chatter from prior chunks.

Recent dialogue tail (reference only, not transcript continuation):
- Speaker 2 (spk_002): So eager to leave, are we?
- Speaker 2 (spk_002): Kill them, men.
